title: Level 794
wikitext:
__notoc__[[Category:Cluster I]][[Category:Dangerous]][[Category:Levels]]{{background|file=wwwwfw.png}} {{CSS|content= @media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine) { body { zoom: 0.9; } }} {{User:A4f7/4}}{{CSS|content=.resizable-container { font-family: Times New Roman; }}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:The Abyss}}{{Class 4|class=4e - Environmental|security=Unstable|entity=Several Non-Entity Hazards|hexagon=4}} ==Overview== {{Blurimage|file=rrrrrrrrrr.png|caption=The most famous photograph of Level 794}} Level 794, also known as “Abyss,” primarily consists of an extremely long, linear plank-like structure made of mahogany. The surface of the plank is covered with an incomplete layer of waterproof paint, which is unevenly distributed, in some areas forming a continuous protective film, while in others leaving the wood fibers exposed. The overall width remains consistently around one meter, allowing a person to walk normally without assistance. The wooden structure forms a highly complex branching network, resembling the planar road systems of many cities. Only a small number of paths are derived routes created to connect two duplicate units. When bridge-like replicated units appear within the structure (all is an imitation of the river-crossing bridges), only the upper supporting components are represented, in the form of thin birch rods, while the rest of the bridge details are absent. The visual presentation of the wooden structure is strongly dependent on the topological complexity of the observer’s location. In areas of lower structural complexity, the visible forward distance is significantly reduced, sometimes exhibiting effects similar to visual compression or fogging. Conversely, in regions with dense branching and numerous nodes, the field of view extends. This phenomenon also affects the output of photographic devices. Individuals walking on the wooden structure are fully subject to standard gravitational effects; no weakening or directional anomalies in gravity have been observed. As a result, in areas with noticeable slope variations, physical exertion increases significantly, and prolonged movement may lead to fatigue accumulation and reduced motor function. Furthermore, some slope transitions are poorly telegraphed, increasing the risk of tripping or falling. The wooden structure cannot be destroyed through violent means, but it can be lifted when attempting to alter it. The resulting pieces tend to be roughly square in shape, meaning each attempt yields only medium-sized, nearly square sections. These fragments can be recombined with other planks at non-primary surfaces and, in optimal conditions, used to construct a temporary platform. The wooden structure possesses active growth capabilities, with growth patterns morphologically similar to those of woody plants. Growth is triggered by the previously mentioned removal-induced gaps and duplication, and occurs at extremely high speeds, exceeding a total length increase of 25 kilometers within a single hour, far surpassing any known natural woody growth. If any object becomes caught within it, the object will be transported to another location on the structure. When attempting to obtain nutrition using non-natural food sources on the wooden structure, a series of highly chained and unpredictable microscopic changes will occur. These changes resemble a cascading butterfly effect, where minor initial actions rapidly trigger multi-layered environmental feedback. These effects compound and amplify each other, ultimately destabilizing the originally controlled conditions and causing the attempt to fail. ==Second Void== {{Blurimage|file=black.jpg|caption=Photograph of Second Void}} Second Void refers to a vast and immeasurable expanse located beyond the boundaries of the wooden structure. Visually, this region exists in a state of near-absolute pitch blackness; its darkness manifests not merely as a lack of visible light reflection or scattering, but further exhibits a highly anomalous light-absorption property. All currently known light sources are incapable of producing any effective illumination within this area. Second Void exhibits an extreme degree of fragmentation, consisting of innumerable discrete segments with indistinct and unstable boundaries. These segments may manifest in a wide variety of forms, including tangible material structures, abstract conceptual constructs, higher- or lower-dimensional spaces, dark matter-like phenomena, and pure energy states. Additionally, physical laws within these segments are frequently inconsistent and may vary unpredictably. In certain regions, conventional reality is entirely supplanted by structures analogous to virtual environments, within which standard physical and perceptual frameworks cease to apply. Individuals located within the Second Void generally suffer high-risk neurological and psychological damage, most of which is permanent and irreversible. This is due to the existence of various invisible spatial lacerations caused by the local phenomena, spatial or conceptual ruptures that cannot be detected by any senses. The human brain appears fundamentally incapable of processing or reconciling these anomalies, resulting in progressive and irreversible cognitive destabilization. Despite the overwhelmingly random and unpredictable nature of nearly all aspects of Second Void, its communication properties exhibit a singular and consistent stability. At any location within the region, individuals are able to establish bidirectional communication with external locations or other levels using a wide range of communication devices. No known mechanism accounts for this phenomenon, and it appears to occur independently of any medium. ==Phenomena== ===Triangular Plank Anomaly=== {{Blurimage|file=triplanky.png|caption=Triangular Plank Anomaly}} This phenomenon refers to the wooden structure rapidly replicating into three copies within a short period of time. The initial manifestation is the wooden structure becoming transparent, followed by its width expanding to three times the original. It then separates into three individual planks, each forming straight segments that intersect with the extensions of the other two at sixty-degree angles. At overlapping regions, voids or partial absences may appear. The former occurs only when two replicated planks overlap. Just before the three planks fully separate and stabilize, the area briefly transforms into a high-risk zone. Due to occupying the space of Second Void, the wooden structure exhibits delayed overlap similar to the normal behavior of that space. As a result, geometric states from different points in time may coexist at the same location. Objects within this region may be forcibly relocated in an instant to any of the three planks, or be segmented and dispersed across different branches. ===Door=== {{Blurimage|file=watert.png|caption=Door Phenomenon(2)}} This phenomenon is extremely rare. It primarily manifests as a wooden door standing vertically on a walkable wooden surface. The door is typically of a standard rectangular shape, with a square opening in its upper half. Directly looking at the door causes severe visual distortion, with the effect being most intense on the door itself. Opening the door carries a certain probability of revealing a different scene. The most common is a multicolored body of water, whose motion is described as similar to tides. Individuals are able to enter this space; however, there is a high probability of losing spatial connection with the original structure. Those who become disconnected cannot be tracked or located. ==Entities== The Whisper is the only known entity associated with Level 794. It possesses no physical form and is more accurately described as an acoustic phenomenon or a source of cognitive interference. Individuals are capable of engaging in apparent dialogue with it, and it typically responds. However, when ignored, it persistently repeats phrases such as “Come with us,” “Be not afraid,” and “We are only trying to help,” among other contextually inappropriate reassurances. These utterances appear to lack any clear purpose or coherent intent. ==Miscellaneous== Pursuant to a widely circulated hypothesis, Level 794 was, for a period of time in the 2000s, merged with Level 642 and two other unidentified levels whose designations have yet to be verified. This state of fusion is said to have persisted for a long time, only separating again in recent years. The theory suggests that, due to the relatively closed nature of interconnectivity between all four areas, while still allowing limited passage, the existence of this complex went unnoticed. There is currently no evidence to support this. [[file:7rrrre.png|center|300px|link=]] ==Attached Note== The note was discovered on an unknown date in mid-December 2025. Based on investigations, it has been confirmed to be associated with this level. The content is as follows:
Recovered Note:
''Somewhere In Where''

According to my unreliable memory, there weren’t this many things here for others to speak of a few years ago. Back then, this place was nothing more than a monotonous wooden plank. Or maybe I remember it wrong, after all, I never personally experienced this place at the time. This dark, unfathomable abyss, all I could see was this vague, elusive plank, stretching endlessly toward a destination unknown to me, toward that which never ends, toward that point where no one can come and go freely. Here, every step is suffering. I have walked for so long, for so far, that even I can no longer keep count, yet there is no way to replenish anything, not even to entertain myself. Right now, I wish I could hold something that brings joy, even a toy would suffice. Though there are clearly many better options, I suppose having anything at all would already be enough. When a person is trapped in such monotony, with nothing but a desolate path before them, there is nothing left to hope for, nothing but despair. Perhaps I truly do possess the capacity to step into that bottomless, almost tangible expanse of black desolation. I once accidentally stepped onto it, proving it is not void, but a forbidden ground that can be trodden upon. Yet I do not wish to risk myself. I am that cowardly, for I do not know what unknowns lie ahead, and I fear they may bring about my death. I hope my assumptions are entirely wrong, that reality would overturn them, but even after exhausting all my will, I have gained nothing, nothing but that endless blackness. Naturally, this has left me trapped here. Aside from that eerie whisper of unknown origin, I am left only with my solitude. Perhaps, at some distant dawn in the future, I may finally encounter the opportunity to escape this mire, but it will certainly not be today. At this moment, I am nothing more than a dying, ridiculous madman curled within the shadows. And how could the cold hands of fate so easily grant me such cheap mercy, or, it does not even know my name?
==Entrances== *Passing through the darker areas of [[Level 305]] will lead you here. *After contracting and recovering from snow blindness in [[Level 420]], there is a chance to arrive at this location. ==Exits== *Falling to a great depth will transport you to [[Level 420]] or [[Level 630]]. *Carrying a glass shard provides a chance to reach [[Level 305]] or [[Level 959|Level 9*9]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial=+ Author Information|final=- Author Information|body= Original Number: Level -2(Level -2C).

Actually I rewrote this piece because I wanted to imitate my previously rejected rewritten version of Level 12 and relive that feeling. But in the end, this mess took me 24 hours and still didn’t turn out well, it’s ridiculously bad. Still, I hope you all like this work.
